component.name: AppHeader
component.description: Full-width top navigation bar. Fixed height (90px per Figma spec). Contains three slots: left logo, centred search field, and right area (nav links + user dropdown). No routing logic — pass nav items and callbacks as props.
component.figmaNodeId: none — no Figma component set exists

when: Every page that requires global navigation and search

antiPatterns.scenario: Using AppHeader inside a page section or Card
antiPatterns.reason: AppHeader is a full-width page-level organism; nesting it breaks its width and positioning semantics.
antiPatterns.alternative: Place AppHeader at the root layout level, above the page content container.

antiPatterns.reason: The component has no router dependency by design — keeping it pure makes it portable.
antiPatterns.alternative: Wrap the href links at the consumer level with your router's Link component, or handle navigation in the onUserClick callback.

purpose.default: Elevated background bar with logo, search, nav links, and user dropdown.

states.description: Static bar — interactive states are limited to nav link hover and user button hover.

ariaAttributes: aria-current="page" on the active nav link

keyboardInteractions.action: Cycle focus through nav links and the user button

compositionPatterns.name: full-page-layout
compositionPatterns.description: AppHeader at the top, followed by a page content Stack, composing the full-width application shell.
